methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen, a Series C climate and energy technology company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham, has raised $380M to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $1.0B. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies, with one public comparable (LNZA). Named peers include KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Amogy ($700M, Series C), and Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B, Series B), with the peer set ranging from $120M to $6.0B.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.7x multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implies $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company cohort yields a median of $500M, reflecting wide dispersion in the climate-tech funding environment. The peer median sits materially below Electric Hydrogen's last valuation, likely reflecting the company's relative stage advancement and capital intensity relative to the broader sample.

The triangulated range spans $425M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median estimate of $1.1B. This analysis supports a fair value in the $1.0B–$1.7B band, consistent with the company's current post-money. Key uncertainties include the valuation dispersion within the peer set and the divergent signals from stage-based versus peer-median methodologies, both reflecting the nascent and heterogeneous nature of frontier energy technology valuations.
